news.title: Comstock Resources (CRK) Q1 Earnings Lag Estimates
news.text: Comstock Resources (CRK) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.15 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.23 per share. This compares to earnings of $0.18 per share a year ago.

news.title: While Asia and Europe scramble for natural gas, the US glut has nowhere to go
news.text: The war with Iran has boosted prices of globally traded natural gas by throttling exports from the Gulf. In ​West Texas, gas is so abundant that some producers must pay to have it taken away.

news.title: Europe's LNG Refill Race: Tailwind for U.S. Natural Gas?
news.text: Europe's low gas storage and global LNG disruptions could boost demand for U.S. exports, putting EQT, Cheniere Energy and Comstock Resources in focus.

news.title: Comstock Resources: Increasing The Bet On The Western Haynesville
news.text: Comstock Resources, Inc. secures a natural gas supply agreement with NextEra Energy for data center power. Cost reduction efforts in Western Haynesville are ongoing. Low production and transportation costs support CRK's high margins relative to peers.

news.title: COMSTOCK RESOURCES, INC. ANNOUNCES SELECTION OF WESTERN HAYNESVILLE SITE TO HOST POWER GENERATION HUB
news.text: FRISCO, TX, March 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Comstock Resources, Inc. ("Comstock" or the "Company") (NYSE: CRK) announced today that the Western Haynesville will host the recently announced Texas Natural Gas-Fired Power Generation Hub in Anderson County Texas. The selection was announced on March 20, 2026, by the United States Department of Commerce in connection with Japan's $550 billion investment commitment to the United States as part of the U.S.- Japan trade deal.

news.title: Comstock Resources: Attempting To Rebuild Production With Increased Spending
news.text: Comstock is increasing its 2026 capex budget to around $1.45 billion to help rebuild production. Despite some cash burn in 2025, its production decreased by approximately 15% year-over-year. I now project $463 million in 2026 cash burn with a 5% year-over-year production increase (although exit rate production should increase significantly more).

news.text: FRISCO, TX, Feb. 11, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Comstock Resources, Inc. ("Comstock" or the "Company") (NYSE; NYSE Texas: CRK) today reported financial and operating results for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025.

news.title: Turning A Multi-Trillion-Dollar AI Tailwind Into Real Returns
news.text: Massive hyperscaler CapEx - $560B in 2024 - drives a QE-like cycle, fueling real-economy demand for data centers, power, and infrastructure. AI-driven CapEx is forced and persistent, creating bottlenecks in energy, grid, and materials, favoring infrastructure and energy suppliers. Amazon and Alphabet are executing aggressive CapEx plans, internally funded, but face higher depreciation and operational risks.
